programme.title: Travel Man: 48 Hours in...
programme.desc: Joe Lycett sets off on more mini-breaks. Sarah Millican joins Joe in the Lithuanian capital Vilnius, where hot air ballooning, cepelini dumplings and gira, and the Lukiskes Prison await.

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.sub-title: Handlebar, Casa Roma and The Black Pearl
programme.desc: Chef Ramsay returns to some of the most memorable restaurants in the past to see if they've implemented positive changes.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: Sarah Beeny's New Life in the Country
programme.desc: This special compilation episode follows Sarah, her husband Graham and their four boys as they trade London for a new life in rural Somerset, and explores the best bits of their new rural family life.

programme.title: Narrow Escapes
programme.desc: Canal cruising returns, as more boaters enjoy new adventures on our waterways. Claire takes on a tugboat restoration. Roger and Jo host a paint-and-sip event in the Cotswolds.

programme.title: Sun, Sea and Selling Houses
programme.desc: Emotions run high in Almería, as Sharon and Steve Garner find Stephen and Andrew a new home. And the Aldertons help childhood sweethearts Kevin and Liz relocate to the Costa Blanca.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Musician Donagh is taking the giant step of relocating to the vibrant Spanish city of Valencia. He calls on the house hunting skills of Scarlette Douglas to find him the perfect city pad.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Craig Rowe's helping Claire from Bedfordshire find a holiday home in the heart of Mallorca's capital, Palma. Claire has a budget of £190,000 and Craig has five great properties to show her.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Retirees Liz and Tony want to escape the British winters and explore the beautiful island of Mallorca. Can Jean Johannson find them the perfect Balearic bolthole for their £190k?

programme.title: Chateau DIY
programme.desc: Inspired by Bridgerton, Vivienne and Simon create their boldest bedroom yet. Nick and Nicole risk it all building a concrete arch. A deadline and clouds loom for Jane and Rory.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Vicky and Carl have brought their retirement plans forward and are now ready to move to Spain. Jean Johansson shows them what their £70k can buy on the southern Costa Blanca.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: It's the first day of the competition in Worcestershire and fitness enthusiast Claire Wier is producing a low-fat menu for her guests, Tim, Teresa, Chris, and Pippa. Claire remains confident and oblivious all evening, despite her guests moaning about the lack of cream, fat, oil and butter in her meal. As if healthy eating wasn't enough, Claire has her guests working up their appetite with an aerobic workout.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: On day two of the competition in Worcestershire beauty pageant queen and dinner party virgin Pippa Langhorne throws a sophisticated `Midsummer Night's Dream' evening for her guests. Pippa's fishy starter doesn't get a great response from shellfish-hater Claire or oddball Tim, who claims fish are his friends. The main goes down well with foodie Teresa, who thinks it very flavoursome, but not so well with moaner Chris.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: It's day three in Worcestershire and party boy publican Tim Lawson is hoping to win over his guests with a raucous country-themed blow-out. He's hoping his wild night will be enough to take the lead ahead of calorie counter Claire Wier and wannabe opera star Pippa Langhorne. Will Tim's novel take on a prawn cocktail be enough to impress?

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: On day four in Worcestershire, Teresa Cox is hoping to win over her guests with a rich, sumptuous feast. Will they appreciate the fine food, especially fitness enthusiast Claire Wier, who will probably faint at the amount of cream Teresa is planning to use? With the group feeling jaded after a wild night at publican Tim Lawson's dinner party, what will serial moaner Chris Sheppard make of Teresa's efforts?

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Chris Sheppard throws a sophisticated black-tie-themed evening for his guests. He's got a job on his hands to bring harmony back to the group after Pippa stormed out of Teresa's party when Tim had the audacity to claim that her pet dog couldn't speak French. Chris's starter goes down a treat, with everyone agreeing his home-made sweet chilli sauce is the best they've ever tried.

programme.title: The Dog House
programme.desc: Seventeen-year-old farmer Sam seeks a tractor cab companion, preferably one that likes to dance. Can cocker spaniel Araf hit the right note?

programme.title: Steam Train Diaries
programme.desc: We travel from Whitby to Grosmont in time for the railway's steam gala, an engine is transported across land and sea, and walkers take on 'the rail trail'.

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A little boy has a stone up his nose from playing football. A 92-year-old has had a fall by her car. And World War II hero Norman is 95 and has a dangerously low heart rate.

programme.title: Emergency
programme.desc: Emergency returns as a tense four-part serial across the week. As a pedestrian is crushed by a bus and a holiday paraglide goes wrong, can the frontline trauma units save lives?

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: Britain's sharpest armchair critics share more of their sharp, insightful, passionate and sometimes emotional critiques of the week's biggest and best shows on television.

programme.title: Celebrity Gogglebox
programme.desc: What do Mo Gilligan, Babatunde Aléshé, Jon Richardson, Lucy Beaumont, Fearne Cotton, Gok Wan, Miquita and Andi Oliver, Johnny Ball and other famous faces think of our top telly?

programme.title: Night Coppers
programme.desc: The Night Coppers break up a mass brawl and tackle some urban explorers up a crane. And an inebriated nuisance neighbour doesn't want to go home and won't go to bed.

programme.title: Night Coppers
programme.desc: Annie and Jack get more than they bargained for when they try to move some drunken revellers who have been ejected from a bar. Manny carries out a welfare check after police receive a call from an ex-partner. Fran and Matt attend a suspected spiking.

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: The staff at Queen's, Nottingham, are shocked as Sister Lou, one of their own, has a major bike accident. And 57-year-old Stuart's fallen off a ladder and fractured his ankle.

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: The staff at Queen's, Nottingham, are shocked as Sister Lou, one of their own, has a major bike accident. And 57-year-old Stuart's fallen off a ladder and fractured his ankle.

programme.title: Travel Man: 48 Hours in...
programme.desc: Asim Chaudhry joins Joe to spend the weekend in Marseille. Together they visit Hotel Le Corbusier, venture through an underwater museum, and learn some tricks at a world-famous skatepark.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Rookie interceptor Rachael faces a baptism of fire including a chase with a pregnant passenger, and a deluded motorist calls the police to report interceptor Chris for stealing his car.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: A failure to stop turns into a full-blown kidnapping situation. Liam and the team make a shocking discovery when a 14-year-old lad is found in possession of a bag of crack cocaine, cash and a knife.

programme.title: The Proud Rebel
programme.desc: At the end of the Civil War, a Southerner travels north, determined to find a cure for his mute son.

programme.title: The Big Trees
programme.desc: Jim Fallon, an unscrupulous lumber baron goes to northern California to part the Quaker settlers from their giant sequoias. However, the homesteaders are having none of it, and Fallon ends up falling for one of the Friends.

programme.title: Trucking Hell
programme.desc: Simon grapples with a juggernaut that has skidded off the M62 and is leaking diesel. Meanwhile, Rory deals with a mobile library that has been the victim of a shocking arson attack.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: The team tackle some boozers in Barrow whose spirits are high, Duggie and Mark look into a car that has come a cropper and its driver has fled and the cocky occupants of a Mondeo refuse to face the bald facts about their tyres.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: The Operational Support Unit have their hands full when a raid reveals a crowbar-wielding suspect with a house full of drugs, a taser and an offensive mouth. The chopper is scrambled to bring down a runaway burglar.

programme.title: Motorway Cops: Catching Britain's Speeders
programme.desc: Cops spot a car speeding by the Tyne, and when they pull it over, a search uncovers a crack pipe. And PC Martain Anderson stakes out a suspected drug driver.

programme.title: Motorway Cops: Catching Britain's Speeders
programme.desc: A member of the public reports a car crash with two occupants and a driver who stinks of booze. And after a white transit van attempts to ram a police car off the road, it crashes and the occupants bail, leading to a massive manhunt.

programme.title: Police Code Zero: Officer Under Attack

programme.desc: Mulder and Scully investigate a series of bizarre deaths plaguing a small town.

programme.title: Seconds From Death: Caught on Camera
programme.desc: A look at the world of extreme sports, investigating a free climber's lack of fear,showing footage of how an extreme skier's worst nightmare unfolded, and detailing how a sky diver survives when faced with a mid-air medical emergency.

programme.title: Outback Truckers
programme.desc: The past threatens to repeat itself as Steve Grahame and his convoy partner Sy Howells take on rough roads and extreme weather to make it to a remote community before the wet season leaves them completely stranded.

programme.title: Trucking Hell
programme.desc: Crouch incident manger Steve and his team have been called out to a lorry that has caught fire on the extremely busy A1. By the time Steve arrives, the fire has been put out and Callum has pulled the tractor unit onto a low loader.

programme.title: Winter Road Rescue
programme.desc: In Scotland, the chaotic weather sees the small town of Lochcarron effectively cut off from the outside world when a huge landslide brings tonnes of rock and trees crashing down onto a vital road.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: AJ and Richie are in pursuit of a van speeding through narrow country lanes. Jacko and the team box in a van carrying large quantities of cannabis. Plus, sparks fly when dog handler Justin and police dog Elsa capture two burglars on a building site.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: A runaway mugger rams Faulko off the road and puts him in hospital. The team hunts down the driver responsible. Meanwhile Liam locks up a rogue biker with the aid of patience, pepper spray and a heroic passer-by.

programme.title: Black Horse Canyon
programme.desc: Frontier drama about two cowhands who try to capture a wild stallion responsible for luring the mares from a small town into the wild. The duo believe the stallion to be very valuable, especially if it is put to stud, and are keen to capture it as quickly as possible, but they soon encounter fierce competition from an unexpected quarter.

programme.title: Trucking Hell
programme.desc: A luxury coach gives up the ghost on a busy motorway slip road. A straightforward job segues into disaster for Marta. Scott races to recover a damaged trailer before dark descends.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Jon puts his body on the line as he tackles a drunken yob in a domestic dispute, a moonlighting pizza delivery driver is nicked after taking fast food to the extreme and Martin turns detective to catch out a drink driver.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: The caseload includes the discovery of an illegal car operation, a bizarre late-night compost thief, a trucker with a violent temper, a cruel animal blood sport, an unexploded bomb and some very cute calves.

programme.title: Motorway Cops: Catching Britain's Speeders
programme.desc: At night, on the A1 in Newcastle, PCs Jan Simlesa and Marc Crammond spot a driver with his foot down, weaving in and out of lanes and bullying other motorists out of his way. In Sunderland, PCs are on the hunt for a BMW driver with no insurance.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Profiling the work of high-speed police interception units around the country with some of the most dangerous pursuits and shocking footage ever captured. Exploring what it takes to bring order to chaos.

programme.title: Police: Night Shift 999
programme.desc: In Gloucestershire, police officers are called to a late-night break-in at a shop. Thieves targeting cigarettes have raided the premises and escaped at high speed.

programme.title: The X-Files
programme.desc: The agents assist Mulder's former mentor with a case involving a serial killer who claims to be possessed by a demonic force.

programme.title: Seconds From Death: Caught on Camera
programme.desc: Hikers take a deadly trip on a helicopter, a family does the unthinkable on their day out at the safari park, a slip on an icy glacier spells disaster, and a cultural festival turns deadly.

programme.title: Outback Truckers
programme.desc: Brothers and fourth-generation loggers, Adrian and Neil, do their best to stop an inferno raging in Tasmania's bushland that threatens to destroy their livelihood.

programme.title: Trucking Hell
programme.desc: Super-sized transport jobs require even bigger trucks to perform them. Lifting the lid on the heavy recovery business, featuring industrial strength trucks capable of towing and lifting the biggest and heaviest machines on the nation's roads.

programme.title: Trucking Hell
programme.desc: Alpha Recovery join the Trucking Hell team as Kev is tasked with rescuing a 45-tonne mobile crane with a blown engine. Not only is the crane stranded on the A1 at the height of rush hour, it's also marooned in the midst of major road works.

programme.desc: A daily round-up of showbiz news and gossip from around the world, focusing on celebrities, movies, music and entertainment.

programme.title: The Hospital: Life On The Line
programme.desc: Dedicated staff at the NHS Golden Jubilee Hospital near Glasgow provide a lifeline to patients, including a 54-year-old awaits a heart transplant and a 27-year-old born with a condition that effects the chambers of her heart.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Inappropriate Sister
programme.desc: After returning from another disappointing audition, Joey tries to write a play in which he can star. Meanwhile, Rachel gets a date with her neighbour Danny, but is perturbed to discover his extremely intimate relationship with his sexy sister.

programme.title: Bargain-Loving Brits in the Sun
programme.desc: In a brand-new edition, DJ Jessie moves into an apartment with her new girlfriend, and headbanger Jonny Hellraizer's kids join him in the family business of rock 'n roll.

programme.title: Bargain-Loving Brits in the Sun
programme.desc: Bar owner Geoff is in the process of expanding into the letting business with help from his pal Phil, and Pat Earle is fundraising for her BARC charity with a fiercely competitive golf tournament.

programme.title: Car Pound Cops: Give Me My Car Back!
programme.desc: Following the dramatic work of recovery crews as they trace, track and tow vehicles driven illegally on Britain's roads. With access to two recovery companies, featuring the action from the moment they are called in, to the trucks arriving on scene.

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: Near York, a young driver in a stolen car is on a suspected high behind the wheel. The police must make a move before the reckless man can join a busy motorway and cause more mayhem.

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: Drink-driving, drug use and atrocious weather all contribute to trouble for the traffic police as they investigate cases including a driver who is spotted taking drugs whilst behind the wheel of a car transporter.

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: Near York, a young man who has stolen his grandfather's car leads the police into a bizarre and dangerous pursuit that ends in a trip to hospital for the youngster. Meanwhile, police elsewhere track two suspected fuel thieves.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: A serial offender gives officers the runaround, a high-speed pursuit comes to a painful finish, the occupants of a 4x4 need rescuing after overturning on treacherous ice, and the interceptors are called on a mucky mercy mission after two of their own get caught in a sticky situation.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Angus and Sean have to smash down a door in order to save a man's life, veteran interceptor Gordon picks up a gruesome twosome on the M6, and a fleeing Land Rover rams two cop cars in a bid to get away.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Glen rushes to help a woman trapped inside a burning car, Hayley is on patrol during `Black Eye Friday', and Ross and John are called in to help a victim who has been bound and gagged. Meanwhile, Duggie and Gareth deal with a bullock breakout, Steve gets a bad boy racer to phone his mum after misbehaving, and a motorway pursuit is bought to an abrupt end with a perfectly timed textbook shunt.

programme.title: Rogue Claimers
programme.desc: Undercover filming exposes a greedy opportunist boosting his payout by more than £300,000, while dash-cam footage reveals the truth behind a road-rage incident turned fraudulent claim.

programme.title: Home and Away
programme.desc: Sonny returns home from physio - and Eden has to clear music equipment so he can navigate his wheelchair through the house.

programme.title: Home and Away
programme.desc: Mackenzie busts into Tane's hospital room, demanding an explanation for why he broke up with Jo.

programme.title: GPs: Behind Closed Doors
programme.desc: Observational documentary series going into the private world of local GP surgeries. An exclusive look into the every day lives of the doctors who work there, with GPs being asked more and more to cover the areas the hospitals can't.

programme.title: Casualty 24/7: Every Second Counts
programme.desc: Return of the series based at Barnsley Hospital's A&E. A 64-year-old man collapses in reception with a suspected heart attack, and Cerys, 21, struggles to breathe.

programme.title: 999: Emergency Call Out
programme.desc: Paramedic Nathan and special sergeant Hannah respond to a Category One incident of a 16-year-old boy who has started fitting. Meanwhile, in West Malling, paramedic Chelsea must assess the injuries of a cyclist involved in a suspected hit and run.

programme.title: 999 Critical Condition
programme.desc: A cardiac arrest patient is found to also have a perforated bowel, a gas explosion could cost a man his leg, and an investigation gets underway into what is causing a woman's blood pressure to plummet.

programme.title: Skin A&E
programme.desc: Four top dermatologists treat patients for skin conditions such as cysts, lipomas and skin tags that the NHS views as cosmetic, but that can hugely affect the sufferers.

programme.title: Scam City
programme.desc: As millions of tourists descend on the city for the Carnival, it's party time for Rio's scammers as well.

programme.title: GPs: Behind Closed Doors
programme.desc: Dr Razak's first patient of the day is Muriel, an elderly woman who recently suffered a mini-stroke. She is reluctant to take her blood pressure medication because she has an aversion to drugs.

programme.title: Casualty 24/7: Every Second Counts
programme.desc: On a 'Manic Monday' in Barnsley Casualty, a 66-year-old patient has a dangerously high heartrate, while another has the opposite - but equally dangerous - condition.

programme.title: The Hospital: Life On The Line
programme.desc: Patient Margo urgently needs life-saving surgery, in a five-hour operation to fix a faulty heart. Geoff, 86, is at risk of heart failure but open-heart surgery is too risky at his age.

programme.desc: The friends make their New Year resolutions, and Joey tries to arrange for Chandler to kiss Monica at midnight at a New Year's Eve party.

programme.desc: Hayley helps two badly injured bikers, Ross and John haul in a drunken driver who has left a trail of destruction in his wake, Duncan escorts a monster truck through Workington, and Steve apprehends a bad boy racer.

programme.title: Port
programme.desc: The final episode of the musical documentary series presented by Julie Fowlis and Muireann NicAmhlaoibh. Julie and Muireann visit North Uist in the Western Isles of Scotland. Accompanying them from Ireland are renowned piper and bodhrán player Donnchadh Gough and the brilliant musician, Síle Denvir. The Uists have long been noted for their singing and piping heritage and prime exponents of both traditions are assembled here.

programme.title: A History of Britain by Simon Schama
programme.desc: A look at the social and economic changes which took place during the reign of Queen Victoria. Industrialisation had a huge impact, and some believed that the machine age would bring about the destruction of the vital bonds that held communities together. Meanwhile, many women were leaving their traditional roles at the centre of the home and family to find new roles in reshaping society.

programme.title: Muhammad Ali
programme.desc: Cassius Clay worked his way up from amateur boxer to Olympic gold medallist in 1960. Clay turned professional and moved to Miami, where he trained and taught himself how to promote. In 1962 he defeats Sonny Banks in his first fight.

programme.title: Muhammad Ali
programme.desc: After his first victory, Clay won every fight. He is introduced to the Nation of Islam and becomes a confidant of Malcolm X. In 1964, he beats Sonny Liston and becomes heavyweight champion.

programme.title: Muhammad Ali
programme.sub-title: Round Two: What's My Name? Part 1
programme.desc: Cassius Clay joins the Nation of Islam and adopts the name Muhammad Ali. For three years Ali dominated the heavyweight ranks, but in 1967 he refused service for the Vietnam War in the U.S. Army.

programme.title: Muhammad Ali
programme.sub-title: Round Two: What's My Name? Part 2
programme.desc: Muhammad Ali dominated the heavyweight ranks for three years. In 1967, he refused service for the Vietnam War and became one of the most reviled men in America. Ali is stripped of his title.

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Join Gethin Jones, Helen Skelton, Michelle Ackerley and the team for conversation, advice and tips to make your day that little bit easier.

programme.title: Doorbell Detectives
programme.desc: When Shaz receives a package that he didn't order, he turns Doorbell Detective and discovers a man intercepting deliveries on his doorstep, pretending to be him. Former police inspector Anton Sullivan demonstrates a simple doorstep scam on pensioner Beryl and gives her some tips to avoid it happening. And what would you do if you caught a burglar in the act of robbing your house? This is what faced Doorbell Detective Ethan.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin meets someone in Devon who is trading his gym membership for the manual labour of property development! Jacqui is in Norfolk off the beaten path... well sort of. Dion's been put behind bars in Birkenhead.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Roo Irvine and the teams go bargain-hunting in Northamptonshire with experts Gary Pe and Tim Weeks, before heading to auction in Derby. Roo also gets special access to the Royal Crown Derby porcelain factory to learn about centuries of craftsmanship.

programme.title: BBC News at One
programme.desc: The latest national and international news, followed by weather.

programme.title: BBC London News
programme.desc: News, sport and weather from London.

programme.title: BBC News at One
programme.desc: The latest national and international news, followed by weather.

programme.title: Natalie Cassidy: Caring Together
programme.desc: Natalie is really looking forward to this week. Care for older people is close to her heart, shaped by looking after her dad, Charlie, and by years of working alongside older colleagues, including some of EastEnders' most familiar faces.

programme.title: Robson Green's Weekend Escapes
programme.desc: Robson joins singer KT Tunstall in her home territory of Fife as they explore the beaches of her childhood near St Andrews, followed by a sea shanty workshop and jewellery making.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman helps Peggy leave London for a rural Gloucestershire home with views for her, two dogs and horse. And there's a taste of country life from former street food chefs.

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Chris and Amelia are in Barnsley to transform Rea and her daughter India's bland and unsafe patch into a boho-style garden influenced by their love of upcycling, crafting and all things rustic. India would love a fairy area, while mum Rea wants a space to relax in and socialise with friends. Can the team achieve all this on a £5,000 budget?

programme.title: Bridge of Lies
programme.desc: Ross Kemp presents the quiz show where a team of four - Mohsin, Gus, Amir and Ayoub - take on the Bridge of Lies. One by one, they all face the Bridge, choosing who faces each crossing depending on the question category.

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: The brainy quiz returns, but this time host Alexander Armstrong is joined by co-host Ria Lina.

programme.title: BBC News at Six
programme.desc: The latest national and international news, followed by weather.

programme.title: BBC London News
programme.desc: News, sport and weather from London.

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Roman Kemp bring you the stories that matter from across the country.

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Ian's world comes crashing down. Max shocks Cindy.

programme.title: Interior Design Masters
programme.desc: It's the semi-finals, and the three remaining designers head to Brighton and Hove Albion's football stadium to transform two hospitality boxes each - one for a player's family and the other for a corporate client. Top fan Norman Cook, aka Fatboy Slim, pops in for a visit, alongside players Georginio Rutter and Fran Kirby. There is a lot to juggle in this tough challenge, and a place in the final at stake.

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Strictly professional dancer Amy Dowden has some family mysteries to solve. Amy knows that her dad's father, Frank Dowden, was adopted but knows nothing about the family beyond that. She would love to get some answers for her dad. On her mum's side there's a rumour of a murder, but Amy doesn't have any details - was her ancestor the victim or the perpetrator? Amy starts in west Wales to investigate the murder rumour.

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Join Gethin Jones, Helen Skelton, Michelle Ackerley and the team for conversation, advice and tips to make your day that little bit easier.

programme.title: Doorbell Detectives
programme.desc: Cheryl turns doorbell detective when she discovers a dodgy duo taking her kid's bike from her garden, but with the help of other victims, do they manage to crack the case? Former police inspector Anton Sullivan dons a flat cap as he heads to the countryside to highlight rural crime. James loses his patience as his cameras capture numerous fly-tippers, and he eventually decides to confront them.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion's in Derbyshire to meet a couple determined to connect a characterful cottage with a beautiful garden to create their dream home. In Tyneside, Martel is reacquainted with a developer who always offers inspirational advice. Tommy in Stoke, where he is charmed at a house full of Victorian period features.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: The teams are hunting for bargains in Newark, with presenter Christina Trevanion and experts Raj Bisram and Roo Irvine. Christina visits the Harley Gallery on the Welbeck Estate to learn about the 5th Duke of Portland, who commissioned a series of eccentric underground building projects there in the 1800s.

programme.title: BBC News at One
programme.desc: The latest national and international news, followed by weather.

programme.title: BBC London News
programme.desc: News, sport and weather from London.

programme.title: BBC News at One
programme.desc: The latest national and international news, followed by weather.

programme.title: Natalie Cassidy: Caring Together
programme.desc: Natalie heads into one of the most emotional weeks of her course so far, dementia care. On her way to class, she's already thinking about her agent Scott, who was married to Barbara Windsor. Watching their journey with dementia from a distance has stayed with her, and she's keen to find out how Scott looked after Barbara as the disease took over. In college, the group are introduced to dementia care before they leave the classroom.

programme.title: Robson Green's Weekend Escapes
programme.desc: Robson and brother David head to a farm near Hexham for a lesson in jam making. They try making a terrarium - a miniature garden in glass - before heading to their childhood favourite beach, Druridge Bay.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: The presenters take the prospective buyers around to different houses in the country. The desire for more room, cleaner air and less noise drives city dwellers to seek an escape to the country.

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock revisits some of her favourite gardens from over the years. In Chester, she returns to see how Michelle and Andrew's space has grown into a stunning jungle garden. In Cambourne, she catches up with Becca and Marcus, whose plot blends Scottish and Australian influences. Finally, in Petersfield, Charlie discovers how Jeannie and Norman's tranquil garden has become a serene country retreat with a touch of oriental flair.

programme.title: Bridge of Lies
programme.desc: Friends Jake, Maria, Louise and Hannah take on the Bridge of Lies. One by one, they must cross the Bridge, stepping on the truths and avoiding the lies.

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: A twist on the classic quiz show formula, contestants search through the depths of their knowledge in an attempt to score as few points as possible.

programme.desc: The latest national and international news, followed by weather.

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Max's emotions force him into a reckless decision, Yolande covers for Denise as she's pulled in two directions, and George makes a worrying discovery.

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: Six weeks of fraught competition have produced four exceptional amateur cooks, who all stand a chance of being enlisted into the MasterChef hall of fame.

programme.title: Amandaland
programme.desc: Amanda tries to persuade Felicity to give her the money she needs to buy her dream house. Meanwhile, Fi buys some wheels for her business, which transforms her into a white-van man.

programme.title: Only Child
programme.desc: Ken is looking forward to having Beth over for dinner, but when Emily and Tim also turn up, an unexpected party gets under way. As the night progresses, secrets threaten to spill out.

programme.title: A Good Girl's Guide to Murder
programme.desc: Five years ago, a schoolgirl was murdered by her boyfriend. It seemed like case closed, but not according to Pip Fitz-Amobi. She decides to uncover the truth and find the real killer.

programme.title: Doorbell Detectives
programme.desc: Cheryl turns doorbell detective when she discovers a dodgy duo taking her kid's bike from her garden, but with the help of other victims, do they manage to crack the case? Former police inspector Anton Sullivan dons a flat cap as he heads to the countryside to highlight rural crime. James loses his patience as his cameras capture numerous fly-tippers, and he eventually decides to confront them.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion's in Derbyshire to meet a couple determined to connect a characterful cottage with a beautiful garden to create their dream home. In Tyneside, Martel is reacquainted with a developer who always offers inspirational advice. Tommy in Stoke, where he is charmed at a house full of Victorian period features.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: The teams are hunting for bargains in Newark, with presenter Christina Trevanion and experts Raj Bisram and Roo Irvine. Christina visits the Harley Gallery on the Welbeck Estate to learn about the 5th Duke of Portland, who commissioned a series of eccentric underground building projects there in the 1800s.

programme.desc: The latest national and international news, followed by weather.

programme.desc: News, sport and weather from London.

programme.desc: The latest national and international news, followed by weather.

programme.title: Natalie Cassidy: Caring Together
programme.desc: Natalie heads into one of the most emotional weeks of her course so far, dementia care. On her way to class, she's already thinking about her agent Scott, who was married to Barbara Windsor. Watching their journey with dementia from a distance has stayed with her, and she's keen to find out how Scott looked after Barbara as the disease took over. In college, the group are introduced to dementia care before they leave the classroom.

programme.title: Robson Green's Weekend Escapes
programme.desc: Robson and brother David head to a farm near Hexham for a lesson in jam making. They try making a terrarium - a miniature garden in glass - before heading to their childhood favourite beach, Druridge Bay.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: The presenters take the prospective buyers around to different houses in the country. The desire for more room, cleaner air and less noise drives city dwellers to seek an escape to the country.

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock revisits some of her favourite gardens from over the years. In Chester, she returns to see how Michelle and Andrew's space has grown into a stunning jungle garden. In Cambourne, she catches up with Becca and Marcus, whose plot blends Scottish and Australian influences. Finally, in Petersfield, Charlie discovers how Jeannie and Norman's tranquil garden has become a serene country retreat with a touch of oriental flair.

programme.title: Bridge of Lies
programme.desc: Friends Jake, Maria, Louise and Hannah take on the Bridge of Lies. One by one, they must cross the Bridge, stepping on the truths and avoiding the lies.

programme.title: Doorbell Detectives
programme.desc: When Shaz receives a package that he didn't order, he turns Doorbell Detective and discovers a man intercepting deliveries on his doorstep, pretending to be him. Former police inspector Anton Sullivan demonstrates a simple doorstep scam on pensioner Beryl and gives her some tips to avoid it happening. And what would you do if you caught a burglar in the act of robbing your house? This is what faced Doorbell Detective Ethan.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin meets someone in Devon who is trading his gym membership for the manual labour of property development! Jacqui is in Norfolk off the beaten path... well sort of. Dion's been put behind bars in Birkenhead.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Roo Irvine and the teams go bargain-hunting in Northamptonshire with experts Gary Pe and Tim Weeks, before heading to auction in Derby. Roo also gets special access to the Royal Crown Derby porcelain factory to learn about centuries of craftsmanship.

programme.desc: The latest national and international news, followed by weather.

programme.title: Natalie Cassidy: Caring Together
programme.desc: Natalie is really looking forward to this week. Care for older people is close to her heart, shaped by looking after her dad, Charlie, and by years of working alongside older colleagues, including some of EastEnders' most familiar faces.

programme.title: Robson Green's Weekend Escapes
programme.desc: Robson joins singer KT Tunstall in her home territory of Fife as they explore the beaches of her childhood near St Andrews, followed by a sea shanty workshop and jewellery making.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman helps Peggy leave London for a rural Gloucestershire home with views for her, two dogs and horse. And there's a taste of country life from former street food chefs.

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Chris and Amelia are in Barnsley to transform Rea and her daughter India's bland and unsafe patch into a boho-style garden influenced by their love of upcycling, crafting and all things rustic. India would love a fairy area, while mum Rea wants a space to relax in and socialise with friends. Can the team achieve all this on a £5,000 budget?

programme.title: Bridge of Lies
programme.desc: Ross Kemp presents the quiz show where a team of four - Mohsin, Gus, Amir and Ayoub - take on the Bridge of Lies. One by one, they all face the Bridge, choosing who faces each crossing depending on the question category.

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: The brainy quiz returns, but this time host Alexander Armstrong is joined by co-host Ria Lina.

programme.title: BBC News at Six
programme.desc: The latest national and international news, followed by weather.

programme.title: BBC Newsline
programme.desc: Local news from across Northern Ireland.

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Roman Kemp bring you the stories that matter from across the country.

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Ian's world comes crashing down. Max shocks Cindy.

programme.title: Interior Design Masters
programme.desc: It's the semi-finals, and the three remaining designers head to Brighton and Hove Albion's football stadium to transform two hospitality boxes each - one for a player's family and the other for a corporate client. Top fan Norman Cook, aka Fatboy Slim, pops in for a visit, alongside players Georginio Rutter and Fran Kirby. There is a lot to juggle in this tough challenge, and a place in the final at stake.

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Strictly professional dancer Amy Dowden has some family mysteries to solve. Amy knows that her dad's father, Frank Dowden, was adopted but knows nothing about the family beyond that. She would love to get some answers for her dad. On her mum's side there's a rumour of a murder, but Amy doesn't have any details - was her ancestor the victim or the perpetrator? Amy starts in west Wales to investigate the murder rumour.

programme.title: Disaster at Sea: The Piper Alpha Story
programme.desc: With Piper Alpha gone, survivors' evidence becomes key to the truth. The inquiry reveals fatal errors, ignored warnings and failed systems. Will there be justice?

programme.title: Life On the Bay
programme.desc: In blazing sunshine on the Fife coast, three generations of the Wallace family help install a brand new caravan on the steep hillside. The family includes 82-year-old owner and manager Tom and his grandson Steven, and over the course of 40 years the Wallaces have built the business up to 600 caravans and a hotel complex. In this episode, the park's newest residents get the keys to their new holiday home.

Chef Julie Lin discovers bargains in the World Food aisles, and gets the low down on low fat, semi-skimmed and full fat milk. Farmer Jim Smith gets stuck into some sticky sweets.

programme.desc: Wildlife rescue officer Bob has interesting cases to deal with on his round including a bat caught in a light fitting, a stranded collared dove chick and a lost racing pigeon.

programme.title: City Lights
programme.desc: A nostalgic look back at `City Lights', the iconic sitcom that captured the spirit of 1980s Scotland. Surviving cast members Elaine C Smith, Jonathan Watson, Dave Anderson and the show's creator, Bob Black look back at the series' magical moments.

programme.title: City Lights
programme.desc: Bank clerk Willie Melvin is convinced that he could be a bestselling author. He has the ambition, determination and a typewriter. All he needs is talent - a point he has overlooked.

programme.title: Fake Friend: The Ticket Scammer
programme.desc: How a Somerset high-flyer turned his charm and connections into tools of deception - conning his school friends and hundreds of others while living a life of glamour himself.

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy asks why there aren't more three-wheeled cars in the world. James attempts to drive a Toyota Hilux up an active volcano in Iceland, and The Stig rips up the track in the new Bentley Continental Supersports.

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy, Richard and James face their greatest challenge to date as all three are sent to Africa and each told to buy a second-hand car costing no more than £1,500. It must not be a 4x4 and must not be designed in any way to go off-road. Which could be a problem, since their task is then to drive across the wilds of Botswana, negotiating massive salt pans, treacherous rivers and animal reserves.

programme.title: Zara McDermott: Uncovering Rape Culture
programme.desc: Beginning with her own sexual assault, Zara investigates sexism and `rape culture' in Britain. The assault left her with many unanswered questions. Speaking to her friends, it quickly became clear that they all had their own stories, and many believe the problem starts at school.

programme.desc: In the heart of Asia lie vast arid lands. These deserts and dry grasslands are so hostile that they push life to the limit. The animals that live here are some of the earth's toughest.

programme.title: Interior Design Masters
programme.desc: It's the semi-finals, and the three remaining designers head to Brighton and Hove Albion's football stadium to transform two hospitality boxes each - one for a player's family and the other for a corporate client. Top fan Norman Cook, aka Fatboy Slim, pops in for a visit, alongside players Georginio Rutter and Fran Kirby. There is a lot to juggle in this tough challenge, and a place in the final at stake.

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: In the first of a two-part special, Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May are in Africa with a simple mission - to find the definitive source of the Nile. Over the years many explorers claim to have already done just that, but the `Top Gear' trio believe that they can do better by travelling further and faster than any exploration team in history using only grit, ingenuity and three ageing estate cars.

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May continue their epic African odyssey. Having survived insane traffic, lethal mudslides and some distinctly dubious map reading, the trio and their ailing estate cars reach the climax of their quest to find the definitive source of the Nile.

programme.title: Glow Up: Britain's Next Make-Up Star
programme.desc: Ten fresh-faced MUAs are booked to create runway looks for avant-garde designer HARRI. Under the watchful eye of legendary key make-up artist Isamaya Ffrench, the MUAs must recreate a technically challenging look to perfection in just 90 minutes.

programme.title: Glow Up: Britain's Next Make-Up Star
programme.desc: Nine MUAs bring virtual looks to life, creating AR lenses for global platform Snapchat.

programme.title: Clean It, Fix It
programme.desc: Jan has lived in her three-bedroom house for over 30 years, but working seven days a week leaves her little time to clean and organise, and her collection of clothes and shoes are covered in dust. The team has just one day to turn her bedroom into a relaxing sanctuary and give her space to rediscover her love of fashion.

programme.title: The Great British Countryside
programme.desc: Hugh Dennis and Julia Bradbury venture across Britain's biggest county, Yorkshire, from the Moors and the Dales to the coast to discover how Britain's epic past lives on in the most fascinating stories and characters. Hugh takes the controls of a glider, and gets winched down into one of Britain's largest potholes on the end of a rope, past the tallest unbroken waterfall in Britain.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Coming from Ramsgate in Kent and features two teddy bears with their own x-ray plates from a dentist, a handbag made from an armadillo shell, and a valuable collection of Louis Wain pictures.

programme.desc: Paul Martin is joined by Anita Manning and James Lewis at the historic Blackpool Tower Circus. James discovers some terracotta tomb figures that could be over a thousand years old, and Anita gets her hands on a collection of seaside postcards.

programme.sub-title: The Prince and The Birthday Bluesies; The Prince and The Chosen One; The Prince and the Tickly Torment; The Prince and The Ducky Day; The Prince and T

programme.desc: Gumball has a penchant for getting into trouble, but he never seems to learn his lesson. Gumball's best friend is a fish named Darwin, who used to be the family pet until he grew legs and became part of the family.

programme.desc: Gumball must find a way to save his relationship with Darwin after the boys are forced to live in separate rooms./Gumball must help Principal Brown have body confidence about his butt in order to be allowed to do his poem about butts.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: When the Titans' real ages are revealed, they must think and act like teenagers to remain Teen Titans. Inspired by a commercial, the Titans visit Dr Light and Dr Moon to learn how to make extra money.

programme.sub-title: Going Overboard; Tale Of The Wolf Mom
programme.desc: Lana stages a scenario to dismiss Chopov as her Guardian, accidentally endangering the ship. / Lana tries to impress her mum at a magic school but learns to embrace her unique talents after chaos ensues.

programme.desc: After a month of questing, Lana looks forward to a Guild VIP Pass, but upon their return to the Guild, she discovers that she failed to fully declare their heroics. Admiral Lovejoy is about to expel the crew.

programme.title: Goat Girl
programme.desc: The teacher loses her mark book and decides without further ado that the upcoming test will form the basis for the school year's marks. Pure panic breaks out in Gigi's class, but she remains positively excited about the first test of her life.

programme.desc: Mordecai, a blue jay, and Rigby, a raccoon, are groundskeepers at a park. They are best friends, but are lazy and don't let their jobs interfere with their ability to have fun.

programme.title: Supertato

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: Bing and Sula are planting seeds at Amma's when Coco arrives with her new skipping rope. Coco teaches them how to skip, with Sula picking it up quicker than Bing. Just when Bing thinks he has succeeded, he hurts his leg on the watering can. Bing can't understand why he is not as good as Sula, but Coco shows Bing that, with some help, he can learn to skip too.

programme.title: Mojo Swoptops
programme.desc: A rockslide blocks Ted the bus and his passengers in a mountain pass. Mojo's mighty dozer top will make short work of those rocks... if Mojo and Bo can work together!

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: Mackenzie, Jack and Rusty are playing as astronauts on a mission to Mars, but during their journey, Mackenzie keeps going missing and no one knows why, not even Mackenzie.

When the taxi crashes and is finally silenced, Bing and Sula use their imagination to play the game they really want to play.

programme.title: Yakka Dee
programme.desc: Dee encourages a friend to say `digger' and we go on a whirlwind word adventure. We see lots of different diggers: a driving digger, a dumping digger and a dizzy digger. There's a muddy digger, a dozing digger and even a disco digger.

programme.title: Lu & the Bally Bunch
programme.desc: Gus finds his own voice when a talking toy he likes loses its voice. Lu is disappointed to see she's not the only Tara at Costume Day. Barnaby doesn't like the pressure of choosing between Lu and Biba's games. The bugs talk about doing funny voices.

programme.title: Froglets

programme.title: Twirlywoos

programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: Justin, George, Mr Tumble and friends get active in this sporty episode.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Tinky Winky, Dipsy, Laa-Laa and Po follow a trail of flowers that leads them to some very special treasure.

programme.title: The Baby Club
programme.desc: Nigel and Baby Bear welcome babies Jean, Elijah, Noah, Baani, Marta, Evie, and their grown-ups to The Baby Club for a special episode featuring dads and male carers. Inside everyone's bags are beach toys which are explored through discovery, play, song and story. Grown-ups at home are encouraged to take part by hiding baby appropriate beach toys inside a fabric bag or pillowcase for them to reveal and explore with their baby!

programme.title: Tiny Tunes
programme.desc: Lisa and the children sign and sing `Head, Shoulders, Knees and Toes'.

programme.title: What's in Your Plant Pot?

programme.title: Monster Makes
programme.desc: Rebecca is engrossed in her new book, but Murphy needs feeding. To help deal with his hunger, Rebecca works with him to make tiger bread toast. The Mini Monsters serve up some fantastic dishy animal makes, and Rebecca's cousin calls with an animal story of his own. None of this excitement is enough to keep Murphy's tummy quiet. Perhaps the final mega monster make will help - a bottle street.

programme.title: Vegesaurs
programme.desc: When an egg cracks to reveal a baby Avocadodon, Ginger and the Pea-Rexes try to find a fun way to lead the energetic baby back to its herd.

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: During a game of 'work', Dad hires Bluey and Bingo at his drainpipe factory. When Bluey takes charge, Dad is less than impressed with the her no dancing rule.

programme.sub-title: Iona and Elba's Wizardy Windy Day

programme.desc: Emilio's family are from Cyprus, an island nation in the Mediterranean Sea. Emilio is making two Cypriot recipes for his friends to enjoy. The first is pitta bread! The dough is formed and rolled into ovals and placed on a baking tray.

programme.title: Maddie's Do You Know?
programme.desc: Maddie takes her special cameras to a bowling alley and looks behind the lane to see where the pins end up, and how they get put back in the right place. We also see underneath the bowling alley to find out how the ball rolls back.

programme.title: Dog Squad

programme.desc: Aneeshwar and Lizzie meet brown bears and a hazel dormouse.

programme.desc: Oona discovers a beautiful rainbow. Baba has never seen a rainbow and Oona really wants to show it to him. Baba is keen, but easily distracted, so the pace is slow.

The Squirrels admire Duggee's new look and want to learn how to accessorise themselves. The first step for the Squirrels is to find something they'd like to change. The Squirrels each bring a different item: wellies, a T-shirt, headband, helmet and a potato. They grab sequins, patches, feathers and lights, and then showcase what they have all done.

programme.title: Numberblocks

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies are flamenco dancing! Tinky Winky, Dispy, Laa-Laa and Po are surprisingly good.

programme.title: Ranger Hamza's Eco Quest
programme.desc: Ranger Hamza and the Ramblers discover how acorns play an important role in the environment. They find out how tiny acorns can grow into giant oak trees that live for hundreds of years and that acorns are a great food source for animals like squirrels who bury them to eat in winter. On the way, they spot a woodpecker's nest in an oak tree and learn that oak trees support more life than any other tree in the UK - all thanks to the tiny acorn.

programme.desc: Elvis refuses to play football after his lucky stinky socks get washed. Evie B leads the hunt for the smelliest smell in the Den so they can make the socks stinky again.

programme.title: Deal or No Deal
programme.desc: Airing from 2005 to 2009 on NBC, the game show series returns to primetime TV with original host Howie Mandel. A contestant must choose from 26 sealed briefcases containing a marker for various amounts of cash -- from one penny to $1 million. The player then eliminates the remaining 25 cases one by one. The chosen ones are opened and the amount of money inside revealed. After several cases are opened, the player is tempted by the Banker to accept an offer of cash in exchange for not continuing the game and possibly winning a larger sum of money.

programme.desc: As the retreat winds down, one couple make amends while others begin planning for the future. But for one pair, a serious roadblock arises.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: When Claire lets Phil take charge of Halloween, he decides to do something different than her usual fright-fest theme, Jay wants to be Prince Charming for Halloween -- complete with a full head of hair and Cam becomes overwhelmed.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Haley tries to land a job with an influential stylist, while Gloria hires a hot Spanish teacher for Manny.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Bart wages war against Milhouse when they are enrolled in different activity clubs.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer and Marge permit Moe to babysit Maggie after she takes a shine to the bartender.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Once Ned and Homer's bounty hunting partnership takes a turn for the worse, Homer becomes the hunted, as Ned is on the prowl for him after he skips bail. Meanwhile, Marge mistakenly takes a job at an erotic bakery.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: A believer in the mantra finders keepers, a sticky-fingered Bart steals actor Denis Leary's phone and uses it for pranking purposes.

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Peralta's undercover FBI operation ends in a successful mass arrest but when he learns that one mobster has escaped, he enlists Boyle's help to track down the missing criminal.

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Captain Holt is taken by surprise when his long-time nemesis, Deputy Chief Madeline Wuntch, arrives to carry out an inspection of his precinct, and Peralta and Jeffords test the boundaries between 'work friendship' and 'real friendship'.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Phil goes into nesting mode after finding some abandoned duck eggs. Meanwhile, Mitchell worries about Cam's friendship with a group of frat boys.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Things fail to go smoothly when Claire takes Haley and Alex into the office for 'Bring your Daughter to Work Day'. Gloria gets chosen for jury duty.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Alex and Luke both give Claire and Phil reason to be concerned. Things get awkward when Haley and Dylan meet Andy and Beth at the cinema.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: When Phil takes on a luxury home, the family are eager to see what it has to offer. Mitch and Cam sneak in for a night of romance.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.sub-title: Four Great Women and a Manicure
programme.desc: The female members of the Simpson household make a trip to a nail salon and discuss whether a woman can be successful without a man at her side. Marge and Lisa each tell their own parodies of 4 classical stories, including Snow White and the Seven Dwarfs.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: An outbreak of food poisoning at Krusty's restaurant chain is traced back to tainted barley in nearby Ogdenville, settled years earlier by Norwegian immigrants. Ogdenville Barley Mills is promptly shut down and Springfield finds itself swamped by the unemployed.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Krusty's confidence takes a kicking and a resident meets their maker.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer and Bart embark on a father-son bonding ocean voyage, while Marge gives fantasy football a go.

programme.title: Hollyoaks
programme.desc: A teenager's grief leads them down a dangerous path. Warren has a surprise for Mercedes. And are a couple over before they've begun?

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.sub-title: So It Has Come to This: The Simpsons Clip Show
programme.desc: Bart's special April Fools' Day prank seriously misfires when the whole house explodes. Homer ends up in the hospital where a candy machine falls on top of him, sending him into a coma. While clips are shown of past blows to his head, family and visitors debate what to do about Homer.

programme.title: Married at First Sight USA
programme.desc: With Decision Day looming, the couples prepare for the biggest call of their lives. Meanwhile, one bride is blindsided by life-changing news.

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: Britain's sharpest armchair critics share more of their sharp, insightful, passionate and sometimes emotional critiques of the week's biggest and best shows on television.

programme.title: Naked Attraction
programme.desc: Psychology student Alesia is tired of meeting `boys' and hopes to meet a real man in the buff. After 200 dates all over the world, Louise is ready to meet her soulmate.

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: More hopeful singletons visit the restaurant, hoping that love is on the menu. Cameron and politics graduate Joe might be able to leave the singles market. Also, ex-car mechanic Alex could be more interested in his waiter than his date.

programme.desc: Britain's sharpest armchair critics share more of their sharp, insightful, passionate and sometimes emotional critiques of the week's biggest and best shows on television.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: The Simpson clan takes a road trip to a cabin in the woods that Homer and Marge once vacationed at 20 years ago. In a series of flashbacks Homer and Marge reflect on some history from their courtship.

programme.title: The Fury
programme.desc: Thriller in which the psychic son of a government agent is abducted by a bogus colleague of his father who wishes to use his powers for evil and profit. When the father and son fall victim to an apparent terrorist attack, their companion, masquerading as the head of an agency studying psychic powers, seizes the boy. In his quest to save his son, the father enlists the help of a clairvoyant with formidable powers of telekinesis.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: The Rare Breed
programme.desc: The widow of an English cattle breeder takes part in a perilous journey to deliver a Hereford bull to a ranch in Texas.

programme.title: The Siege at Red River
programme.desc: A Confederate agent steals a Gatling gun prototype from the Yankees easily enough, but smuggling it south through enemy territory proves tricky when there's a scout in hot pursuit.

programme.title: 3:10 to Yuma
programme.desc: A drought-devastated farmer is desperate for money and, for $200, agrees to guard a notorious outlaw until the train comes to take him to jail. However, the farmer has not reckoned on his prisoner's psychological wiles and must resist manipulative strategies aimed at permitting him to escape.

programme.title: Charade
programme.desc: A woman returns to her Paris flat to find her estranged husband's body and the CIA demanding answers to some baffling questions. She learns that her husband stole a huge sum of gold during the war and tricked four of his wartime buddies in order to keep it all for himself. The police are convinced that she knows the whereabouts of the loot.

programme.title: Belle
programme.desc: The illegitimate, mixed-race daughter of a British admiral plays an important role in the campaign to abolish slavery in England.

programme.title: Django Unchained Interview Special
programme.desc: Director Quentin Tarantino and some of his cool cast talk about their exciting new western adventure, `Django Unchained'.

programme.title: Django Unchained
programme.desc: A former slave joins forces with the German bounty hunter who freed him and helps hunt down the South's most-wanted criminals, all in the hope of finding his long-lost wife.

programme.title: Tombstone
programme.desc: Gripping account of the battle for law and order in the Old West based on the story of US marshal Wyatt Earp, his friend Doc Holliday and the gunfight at the OK Corral. The Earp brothers ride into the town of Tombstone intent on settling down, but when a murderous gang known as the Cowboys begins terrorising the citizens, a showdown is inevitable.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: The Yellow Mountain
programme.desc: Two gold prospectors persuade the owner of a hitherto worthless mine to reconsider his decision to cease excavation. While on a joint dig with the owner and his daughter, one of the prospectors unexpectedly strikes gold, but the precious discovery creates conflict between the duo and prompts unwanted attention from a rival intent on acquiring the gold for himself, at any cost.

programme.title: Arizona Raiders
programme.desc: Clint Stuart is freed from gaol by army captain Tom Andrews, to help track down a gang of renegade outlaws terrorising Arizona.

programme.title: For Whom the Bell Tolls
programme.desc: Robert Jordan is the idealist who joins the Republicans and is charged with blowing up a strategically important bridge. Among the group aiding him are Maria, with who he falls in love, Pablo, the drunken leader of the group and his wife Pilar, who eventually takes charge.

programme.title: The Battle at Apache Pass
programme.desc: Following Apache Chief Cochise who is attempting to keep peace with Major Jim Colton's cavalry outfit at Fort Buchanan. Things begin to go awry when a stubborn Indian Affairs advisor comes west and stirs up hostilities between the soldiers and Cochise's Chiricahuas with underhanded dealings involving renegade Mogollon leader Geronimo.

programme.title: Deep Impact
programme.desc: Apocalyptic thriller in which life on Earth is threatened by a rogue comet. When a reporter in pursuit of a Washington sex scandal is arrested by the FBI, she discovers that the story is an inconsequential part of a huge cover-up. She soon finds herself in possession of the biggest story in the history of humankind - the planet will be destroyed in one year, unless a risky outer-space mission can deflect the comet.

programme.title: Rescue Dawn
programme.desc: During the Vietnam War, German-born US pilot Dieter Dengler is shot down over Laos and taken prisoner. Tortured and starved, Dieter resolves to escape with fellow prisoners Duane and Gene. When they finally make their daring break into the jungle, the escapees discover that the dense, humid rainforest can be a terrifying prison in itself.

programme.title: M3GAN
programme.desc: M3GAN is a marvel of artificial intelligence, a lifelike doll that's programmed to be a child's best friend. When a brilliant roboticist gives her young niece a M3GAN prototype, it soon becomes violent and does anything to protect the girl.

programme.title: Corsage
programme.desc: Faced with a future of strict ceremonies and royal duties, Empress Elisabeth of Austria rebels against her public image and comes up with a plan to protect her legacy.

programme.title: James Martin's Spanish Adventure
programme.desc: In this episode, James visits Spain's wine region of La Rioja and a mile-long cellar - he also eats tapas in the region's capital of Longrono, explores a Michelin-starred restaurant in a tiny town and a community garden rich in produce, and cooks quail, tapas, tomotada and leeks.

programme.title: Mary Berry Cooks
programme.desc: Mary cooks for a relaxed and informal family get-together, with dishes including a slow-roast shoulder of lamb with rosemary and paprika rub, potato and fennel gratin, and a plum tarte tatin and delicious fruit salad.

programme.title: Mary Berry Cooks
programme.desc: Mary Berry is cooking dishes for a family celebration. She makes it seem almost effortless, with recipes for beef and mushroom stew with mustard mash, salmon en croûte with a foolproof salad, and chocolate and orange panna cotta.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Ree Drummond has money-saving ideas for transforming random cuts of beef into four delicious dishes, including slow cooker Sunday stew, teriyaki beef stir-fry, meat pies and grilled beef salad.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Ree cooks up some crazy and fun ideas for game night food. She prepares pull-apart pizza rolls, over-the-top Hawaiian burgers, taco quesadillas and supersized husky bark inspired by the team's colours.

programme.title: Jamie's 15 Minute Meals
programme.desc: Jamie cooks Swedish meatballs, celeriac and spinach rice, plus pasta pesto, garlic and rosemary chicken.

programme.title: Jamie's 15 Minute Meals
programme.sub-title: Green Tea Salmon and Modern Greek Salad
programme.desc: Featuring green tea salmon, coconut rice and miso greens, plus modern Greek salad, spinach, chickpea and feta parcels.

programme.title: Be My Guest With Ina Garten
programme.desc: Ina Garten is joined by Emily Blunt for a fantastic day of cooking and conversation. Ina welcomes her guest with classic English Cream Scones. Then, Emily makes Turkey Bolognese, delicious alongside Ina's easy Caesar Salad with Parmesan Croutons.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: The remaining bakers take on more challenges set by judges Paul Hollywood and Prue Leith in the nation's most famous tent, in a bid to be crowned star baker.

programme.title: Rachel Allen: Bake!
programme.desc: Rachel shares the recipe for the walnut cake with vanilla buttercream filling that she served at her wedding.

programme.title: James Martin's British Adventure
programme.desc: Following on from his French and American Adventures, James Martin explores the British culinary offerings.

programme.title: James Martin's British Adventure
programme.desc: James' culinary tour of Britain continues in Scotland with a trip to Blair Castle in the Highlands. He is joined by top Scottish Michelin starred chef Tom Kitchin. Their journey begins with an incredible tour of the castle grounds complete with awe inspiring views. James kicks off the cooking with his venison with beetroot and blackberries in the castle's walled garden. Next Tom Kitchin dusts of his wellies and heads in to the woods for a bit of wild mushroom foraging.

programme.desc: Mary cooks for a relaxed and informal family get-together, with dishes including a slow-roast shoulder of lamb with rosemary and paprika rub, potato and fennel gratin, and a plum tarte tatin and delicious fruit salad.

programme.title: Be My Guest With Ina Garten
programme.desc: Ina and actor/director Stanley Tucci kick off their day together with easy oysters rockefeller and martinis; there's a Tucci kitchen takeover when Stanley teaches Ina how to make an incredible Tuscan seafood stew, cacciucco.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off

programme.title: Prue Leith's Cotswold Kitchen
programme.desc: Prue welcomes award-winning chef Tommy Banks as she makes two-tone soups and is joined in the kitchen by her husband John for a goat curd and tomato galette.

programme.title: 24 in 24: Last Chef Standing
programme.desc: After more than 20 nonstop hours, Michael Symon and Esther Choi ask the final chefs to create an elevated lunch and dinner. Judges Antonia Lofaso, Graham Elliot and Jet Tila decide who will win $75,000 and the title of Last Chef Standing.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy visits a local landmark in Massachusetts, a 50 year old diner in Buffalo and a family-run Italian restaurant in Cleveland.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy rolls out for medium-rare done right.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy finds a neighborhood place smoking fresh pork; a restaurant turning pizza into sandwiches; a family place serving chicken paprikash.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: A joint serving more than 20 burgers; a restaurant that slow-smokes barbecued pork; a family-run taqueria.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: Atlanta is the next stop on Adams culinary tour, where he attempts to finish a 13-pound Carnivore Pizza. However, this is one challenge he'll need some help with.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: The next stop on Adam Richman's culinary tour of the United States is Boston, where he encounters the giant Eagle's Challenge Burger. Does it come with a giant soda?

programme.title: Cake Boss
programme.sub-title: Crabs, Communion and a Comeback Pastry
programme.desc: It's all hands and claws on deck as a local marina orders a crab cake to kick off the season. However, there's a catch, they want it to move.

programme.sub-title: Miss Richfield, Matryoshkas and a Melt Down
programme.desc: Miss Richfield 1981, returns to Carlo's. She asks Buddy for a cake as colourful as herself for New York City's Gay Pride Family Night.

programme.title: Prue Leith's Cotswold Kitchen
programme.desc: Prue welcomes award-winning chef Tommy Banks as she makes two-tone soups and is joined in the kitchen by her husband John for a goat curd and tomato galette.

programme.title: Mary Berry Cooks
programme.desc: Mary Berry is throwing a casual weekday supper. Her Mediterranean platter of houmous, bread sticks, roasted vegetables and a tomato and mozzarella salad is perfect food for sharing on a summer's evening.

programme.title: Mary Berry Cooks
programme.desc: For Mary's outdoor party the dishes are best served cold; salmon fillets with herb sauce, ham with homemade chutney and fillet of beef with garlic and mustard cream. To accompany the cold meats and fish there are two salads.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Ree shares the epic tale of opening day at The Mercantile while making three get-ahead dinners. She cooks up butternut mac and cheese, Tuscan bean soup and ready-to-go chili packets to turn into a trio of delicious standbys.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Ree shares the good stuff with the chocolate lovers in her life, starting with a triple chocolate tiramisu for her best friend. Then, with her son's help, she makes crispy chocolate dippers to use in a crazy chocolate shake.

programme.title: Jamie's 15 Minute Meals
programme.desc: Grilled steak, ratatouille and saffron rice, plus blackened chicken and San Fran quinoa salad.

programme.title: Jamie's 15 Minute Meals
programme.desc: Jamie rustles up pork steaks, Hungarian pepper sauce and rice, plus killer kedgeree, beans, greens and chilli yoghurt.

programme.title: Be My Guest With Ina Garten
programme.desc: Ina and actor/director Stanley Tucci kick off their day together with easy oysters rockefeller and martinis; there's a Tucci kitchen takeover when Stanley teaches Ina how to make an incredible Tuscan seafood stew, cacciucco.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: This week it's time to bake bread, and the remaining bakers have a lot to prove. They put their own twist on a classic Italian focaccia in the Signature and head slightly further east in the technical, making a Greek-inspired snack..

programme.title: Rachel Allen: Bake!
programme.desc: Rachel bakes carrot cake with orange cream cheese icing, and meets Mich Turner, couture wedding cake-maker to the stars.

programme.title: James Martin's British Adventure
programme.desc: James and Tom are making their way to Edinburgh but before they can do that, James makes a detour to Edradour whisky distillery in Pitlochry where he makes a delicious whisky chicken with wild mushroom and mustard sauce. Once they've reached Edinburgh, Tom shares his local knowledge by visiting one of his favourite suppliers, Welch Fishmongers. He learns the secret to their amazing smoked salmon which James uses for a salmon mousse with cucumber and dill pickle and melba toast.

programme.title: James Martin's Great British Adventure
programme.desc: The time has come to cross the border from Scotland to England and where better to begin this leg of the journey than James' home country of Yorkshire. Here he's joined by Michelin starred chef Lisa Allen. His first stop is Whitby where he's popping into his favourite fish and chip shop before cooking tikka mackerel buns with fennel and apple salad for the crowds at Whitby bandstand. Lisa is paying a visit to The Black Swan.

programme.title: Be My Guest With Ina Garten
programme.desc: Ina Garten welcomes stage and screen actor Laura Linney to her home with a fruity blueberry ricotta breakfast cake; in honour of Laura's Southern heritage, Ina teaches her how to make shrimp boil with a twist.

programme.title: Paul Hollywood City Bakes
programme.desc: Paul gets a guided tour around Cape Town's best baking hotspots from fellow Food Network star Siba Mtongana. Later, Paul creates two bakes of his own: Earl Grey milk tart and BBQ meat pasties.

programme.title: Prue Leith's Cotswold Kitchen
programme.desc: Prue welcomes award-winning chef Tommy Banks as she makes two-tone soups and is joined in the kitchen by her husband John for a goat curd and tomato galette.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Chicken and waffles; double chili cheeseburgers; steak sandwiches with jalapeno pesto; smoked pork chops with applesauce.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Sauteed rib-eye with french fries; beef heart; fresh bread; schnitzel.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Viewer favorites.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Handmade patties with fried green tomatoes; homemade bread; scrapple.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: Adam takes on the hottest curry in the country at New York's Brick Lane Curry House.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: Adam Richman heads to New Orleans, where he takes on a plate of 15 dozen oysters at Acme's Oyster House. Did he remember to bring a knife?

programme.title: Cake Boss
programme.desc: Buddy Valastro and his cake crew are back and whipping up amazing cake creations. They prove that no matter how sticky the situation, the family that bakes together stays together.

programme.title: Cake Boss
programme.desc: Buddy receives a special order: his long-time employee, Nikki, is getting married. But being around cakes for years doesn't mean Nikki knows what she wants.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It's main course day, and after the disastrous fish course Mary Ellen McTague, James Durrant and Mark Ellis are all desperate to impress the veteran judge.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It's Mary Ellen McTague, James Durrant and Mark Ellis' last chance to impress with their desserts as at the end of today's cooking one chef must leave the competition.

programme.desc: The two remaining North West chefs cook their four courses again for the judges Prue Leith, Oliver Peyton and Matthew Fort, who must decide which chef will win a place in the finals.
